description: Promotional posts offering bundled food items with free additions, emphasizing value and urgency through limited-time offers and drive-through convenience.
structural_recipe: Lead with the primary bundled item (e.g., large chicken meal), follow with free add-ons (e.g., 2 junior burgers free), highlight the vehicle/drive-through angle, set clear expiration date, use Arabic mixed with emojis for emphasis, include call-to-action (اطلب الحين/order now), deploy fire emoji and heart emojis for urgency and brand affinity.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers value practical deals and convenience; drive-through framing aligns with car culture and mobility-first lifestyle. Limited-time offers (حد معين تاريخ) create urgency and FOMO. Free additions amplify perceived value without reducing margin perception. Arabic-language packaging builds local trust and cultural relevance.
transplantation_caveats: Drive-through emphasis may be less effective in regions without strong car-centric dining culture
Arabic-language copy requires native fluency to avoid tone mismatches
Expiration dates must comply with local advertising standards and consumer protection laws
Bundle sizing and portion psychology may differ across price-sensitive markets
cultural_constraints: Must avoid promotional language that conflicts with local meal timing (prayer times, Ramadan fasting windows)
Food imagery should respect local dietary preferences and halal certification expectations
